title: salvia officinalis subsp. gallica
intro: Salvia officinalis subsp. gallica, also known as French lavender sage or purple sage, is a subspecies of the Salvia officinalis plant, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe digestive issues and skin irritations. The health benefits of Salvia officinalis subsp. gallica include reducing stress and anxiety, improving cognitive function, and supporting respiratory health. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
